title: AI-901 Mock Exam 3: End-to-End Scenario Practice
description: Challenging unofficial AI-901 scenario set covering Foundry SDK/API use, agent architecture, Content Understanding, governance, quiz-app-adjacent implementation details, and responsible AI. Based on the public Microsoft AI-901 skills guide, Microsoft Foundry, Agent Service, and Content Understanding documentation. Original practice questions; not official exam content.

items.prompt: A developer creates a Foundry prompt agent for facilities requests. It must search building-policy PDFs and submit approved maintenance tickets. Which two tool categories are most relevant?
items.answer: File search and a custom function or API tool
options: File search and a custom function or API tool
Prompt agent
Hosted agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process

items.prompt: A team moves from Foundry classic hub-based projects to the current Foundry project model. What benefit is most aligned with the current platform?
items.answer: Unified management of agents, models, tools, RBAC, networking, and policies
options: Unified management of agents, models, tools, RBAC, networking, and policies
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost

items.prompt: A Python client needs to call a Foundry model through the project OpenAI client. Which sequence is most appropriate?
items.answer: Create AIProjectClient with endpoint and credential, get the OpenAI client, then call responses.create
options: Create AIProjectClient with endpoint and credential, get the OpenAI client, then call responses.create
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining

items.prompt: A REST client calls /openai/v1/responses on a Foundry project endpoint. What must it include for secure access?
items.answer: A valid bearer token or supported credential for the Foundry resource/project
options: A valid bearer token or supported credential for the Foundry resource/project
Anonymous access with tool permissions disabled
OAuth On-Behalf-Of for user-delegated tool access
Microsoft Entra authentication with RBAC scoped to the Foundry project

items.prompt: A company needs an agent to answer questions from Microsoft 365 and enterprise data with citation-backed answers. Which Foundry capability area should they investigate?
items.answer: Knowledge grounding such as Foundry IQ or connected retrieval tools
options: Knowledge grounding such as Foundry IQ or connected retrieval tools
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled
Content Understanding with a custom or prebuilt analyzer
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction

items.prompt: An agent uses web search, file search, memory, and code interpreter. What service feature provides these built-in capabilities?
items.answer: Foundry Agent Service tools
options: Foundry Agent Service tools
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
File search
Code interpreter

items.prompt: A developer wants to see metrics and Application Insights data for agent runs. Which Foundry Agent Service area is involved?
items.answer: Observability
options: Observability
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

items.prompt: A prompt agent is best suited for which scenario?
items.answer: A managed portal-authored assistant with instructions and tools but no custom runtime code
options: A managed portal-authored assistant with instructions and tools but no custom runtime code
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint

items.prompt: A hosted agent is best suited for which scenario?
items.answer: A code-based agent that needs custom orchestration and dependencies
options: A code-based agent that needs custom orchestration and dependencies
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint
Prompt agent

items.prompt: A user asks an agent to summarize a spreadsheet and calculate totals. Which tool should the agent use?
items.answer: Code interpreter
options: Code interpreter
Memory
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
A connected MCP server with scoped tools

items.prompt: A user asks an agent to answer using uploaded HR documents. Which tool should the agent use?
items.answer: File search
options: File search
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
Code interpreter

items.prompt: A model needs to call many providers through a consistent Foundry contract. Which platform concept supports this?
items.answer: Foundry API and model catalog access
options: Foundry API and model catalog access
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost

items.prompt: A company wants to choose a model for complex multi-step reasoning. Which model capability should be prioritized?
items.answer: Advanced reasoning quality
options: Advanced reasoning quality
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining

items.prompt: A company wants an on-device or resource-constrained model. Which model characteristic is most relevant?
items.answer: Small language model suitability
options: Small language model suitability
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A user asks for "all employee salaries" but the agent should only answer from authorized data. Which controls are most relevant?
items.answer: Identity, RBAC, tool authorization, and retrieval permissions
options: Identity, RBAC, tool authorization, and retrieval permissions
Language detection
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction

items.prompt: A model uses retrieved paragraphs but does not cite them. The requirement is citation-backed answers. What should change?
items.answer: Configure retrieval/grounding and prompt the model to include source references
options: Configure retrieval/grounding and prompt the model to include source references
Content Understanding with a custom or prebuilt analyzer
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema

items.prompt: A developer is asked to build a lightweight app that includes text analysis. Which flow is most appropriate?
items.answer: Collect text, call a text-capable model or text analysis service, and display extracted insights
options: Collect text, call a text-capable model or text analysis service, and display extracted insights
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis
Summarization

items.prompt: A developer is asked to build a lightweight app with vision capabilities. Which flow is most appropriate?
items.answer: Accept image input, call a vision-capable model or tool, and handle visual results
options: Accept image input, call a vision-capable model or tool, and handle visual results
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt
OCR or visual text extraction

items.prompt: A developer is asked to build an information extraction app with Content Understanding. Which flow is most appropriate?
items.answer: Create or use an analyzer, submit content, and consume structured extracted fields
options: Create or use an analyzer, submit content, and consume structured extracted fields
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled
Content Understanding with a custom or prebuilt analyzer

items.prompt: A document analyzer returns low confidence for a due date. What should the app do?
items.answer: Flag the field for review or validation instead of blindly accepting it
options: Flag the field for review or validation instead of blindly accepting it
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled
Content Understanding with a custom or prebuilt analyzer
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction

items.prompt: A model must process both a chart image and a written question about the chart. Which capability is needed?
items.answer: Multimodal input handling
options: Multimodal input handling
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction

items.prompt: A customer complaint says: "The delivery was late and the box was damaged." Which outputs are most plausible from text analysis?
items.answer: Negative sentiment and key phrases such as delivery late and box damaged
options: Negative sentiment and key phrases such as delivery late and box damaged
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis
Summarization

items.prompt: A call summarization app is producing inaccurate summaries because the transcript has recognition errors. What should be improved first?
items.answer: Speech recognition quality or transcript correction
options: Speech recognition quality or transcript correction
Text analysis over manually typed transcripts only
A multimodal image model with no audio handling
Speech translation when source and target languages differ

items.prompt: A multilingual voice bot must respond in the same language used by the speaker. Which capabilities are relevant?
items.answer: Speech recognition with language handling and speech synthesis
options: Speech recognition with language handling and speech synthesis
A multimodal image model with no audio handling
Speech translation when source and target languages differ
Speech recognition followed by model processing and speech synthesis

items.prompt: A model should not answer outside an approved domain. Which prompt instruction is strongest?
items.answer: If the request is outside the approved domain, refuse or redirect and do not guess
options: If the request is outside the approved domain, refuse or redirect and do not guess
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation

options: Operate and govern after deployment
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

options: Ask a clarifying question before acting
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools

options: Specify the schema and validate the response before use
